lessons.title: 6 PATTERN AND DESIGN
topics: Introduction
Patterns and Designs
The topic discusses patterns and designs around the environment, shaping our emotions,
behaviours and interactions. Patterns influence our experiences through various forms
(architecture, fashion...).
assessment: Observation of learners during pattern making activities
Review of learners’ art works for composition, texture and colour.

lessons.title: 1.7 DRAWING AND PAINTING FROM IMAGINATION
topics: Introduction:
This topic will help learners unlock the power of their imagination, it will give them freedom to
explore their thoughts, emotions, experiences and express them in a visual language. Drawing
and painting from imagination is a creative process that allows learners to tap into their inner
world and bring forth unique and innovative ideas.
activities: out-door (at the market, school premises, outside the classroom

lessons.title: 8 DRAWING AND PAINTING FROM OBSERVATION
topics: Introduction:
Drawing and painting from observation is a fundamental skill for artists, allowing them to
accurately capture the world around them.
This topic explores the techniques and principles of drawing and painting from observation,
helping artists refine their skills and create more realistic and engaging works. By observing and

This topic exposed learners to acquire skills in drawing from observation and represent

lessons.title: 9 DRAWING AND PAINTING FROM STILL LIFE
topics: Introduction
In this topic, the learners will acquire skills such as, creativity, observation and analytical
thinking. Still life drawing and painting is an artistic style that captures the aesthetics and essence
of static objects, often arranged in deliberate compositions. From everyday household items like
fruit and books to symbolic or luxurious objects, artists use light, colour, texture, proportion and
activities: out-door (a stack of arranged rocks, at the scrap metal junk yard, part of an engine
ii. Pencils: Graphite, charcoal, or coloured pencils used for drawing.
assessment: Evaluate Still life drawings based on shape, texture, form, value (light and dark),
works, promoting critical thinking and constructive criticism.
In this sub-topic, different pictures were composed from Still-life drawing and or
painting. The key techniques and principles acquired and applied were, observation,
rendering, composition, colour theory, lighting and shading.

lessons.title: 10 FIGURE DRAWING
topics: Introduction
The topic will discuss the fundamental skills like proportion, pose, gesture and line art. Stick-
Figure introduces the learners to basics on the use of line and shape in order to create simple
imagery.
